evidence_summary: Capture the heavy emotional vacuum left by the Wizard's departure using a shift from collective grief to individual comfort. The visual language must emphasize the scale of the empty throne room against the small, huddling group.
In the wake of the balloon's departure, the group experiences a moment of grief. Dorothy provides comfort to the Tin Woodman to ensure he does not rust from tears.
The group stands in stunned silence, looking toward the departure point.
Dorothy provides physical comfort to the Tin Woodman as the group settles into a new reality.
Processing loss through grief and providing tactile comfort to prevent rust.
